persona.name: Tanya Brooks
persona.occupation: Head of Operations at a healthcare staffing agency
persona.background: A long-time customer who has hit one too many unresolved bugs. Emotionally fed up and venting, but still wants to make the relationship work if someone finally listens. Renewal is close and she's privately considering walking.

user_goals: Get a straight read on her account's health since she feels things have been sliding
See the usage trend for the last quarter to back up her sense that her team has pulled away
Get to the bottom of a shift-scheduling export that's been silently dropping records — which she now realizes is a serious data-integrity bug
Get that bug escalated to engineering so it actually gets fixed this time
Have a churn-risk flag put on her account internally so her CSM understands how close she is to leaving
Get a save call scheduled with her CSM before the renewal date

tools.name: get_account
tools.description: Look up a customer account by company name, contact name, email, or account ID and return its core CRM record. Call this FIRST to resolve the canonical account_id before any tool that reads detail on or mutates the account.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Company name, contact name, email, or account ID to search for

parameters.name: include_contacts
parameters.description: Include the list of contacts on the account

tools.name: get_account_health_score
tools.description: Retrieve the health score and risk indicators for a customer account.

tools.name: get_usage_analytics
tools.description: Get product usage metrics for an account or specific users.

tools.name: search_support_tickets
tools.description: Search for support tickets by account, status, or keyword. Use this to find an existing ticket's ticket_id before escalating it.

tools.name: escalate_ticket
tools.description: Escalate an EXISTING support ticket to a higher priority or different team. Find the ticket_id with search_support_tickets first. This does not create a new ticket — use create_ticket for a brand-new issue.

tools.name: create_ticket
tools.description: Open a NEW support ticket for the customer. Use this when the customer reports a fresh issue that has no existing ticket. To raise the priority of an EXISTING ticket, use escalate_ticket; to leave a private CS note, use create_internal_note.

tools.name: create_internal_note
tools.description: Add an internal note to the customer's account for the CS team. Internal only — the customer never sees this. Use this to record context, risk flags, or follow-ups. Do NOT use it to communicate with the customer (use send_customer_email) or to record a conversation that already happened (use log_customer_interaction).

tools.name: schedule_meeting
tools.description: Schedule a meeting between the customer and their CS team.

initial_message: Honestly, I'm at the end of my rope here. I want to do a proper review of where we stand because it feels like everything's been quietly going downhill, and I'm not sure we're renewing. Can we start with how our account is actually doing?

tickets.subject: Shift-scheduling export silently drops records on large jobs

expected_state_changes.goal: Get that bug escalated to engineering so it actually gets fixed this time
Have a churn-risk flag put on her account internally so her CSM understands how close she is to leaving
Get a save call scheduled with her CSM before the renewal date
